title: Community Mental Health Agencies * Hispanic/Latino Community - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Mental & Behavioral Health > Community Mental Health Agencies * Hispanic/Latino Community 3 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter African American Community Hispanic/Latino Community Native American Community Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF CENTRAL CITY CONCERN PUENTES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers supportive services to the Latino/a/e and Hispanic communality and non-English speakers seeking healing and recovery. Provides bilingual/bicultural alcohol and drug treatment and mental health services, case management, mentoring, access and referral to housing resources, continuing care when treatment is finished, and if necessary referrals for medical, psychiatric, and holistic. Treatment plans are individually tailored and offered by clinicians and staff that share clients' cultural background and language. Treatment address conditions like depression, anxiety, trauma, and other life stressors. Plans include behavioral health services such as Mental health care Group counseling, individual counseling, outpatient addiction treatment, recovery groups, peer support case management, DUII treatment. Eligibility: Individuals who are: -Latinx (monolingual/bilingual) -Adults (over 18 years of age) in need of mental health counseling, substance abuse and/or DUII treatment Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 546-9975 Visit Website More Details PLAZA DE NUESTRA COMUNIDAD EUGENE MAIN OFFICE MENTAL HEALTH CARE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides bilingual mental health evaluations and services including individual, couples, and family therapy. 195 W 12th Avenue Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Age 6 and older; primarily focus is Latino and immigrant population Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-noon and 1pm-6pm, after hours appointments available by request Email (541) 687-2667 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PLAZA DE NUESTRA COMUNIDAD SPRINGFIELD EDUCATION OFFICE BEHAVIORAL HEALTH & EDUCATION SERVICES MENTAL HEALTH CARE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides bilingual mental health evaluations and services including individual, couples, and family therapy. 1621 Centennial Boulevard Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Age 6 and older; primarily focus is Latino and immigrant population Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-noon and 1pm-6pm, after hours appointments available by request Get Directions Visit Website More Details PLAZA DE NUESTRA COMUNIDAD MENTAL HEALTH CARE PLAZA DE NUESTRA COMUNIDAD MENTAL HEALTH CARE
